topic.name: Cricket History and Players
topic.description: A collection of documents detailing the history of cricket, notable players, and significant matches in the sport.

1: Joe Small was a West Indian cricketer who played in West Indies' first Test.
He scored the first half century for a West Indies player in Test cricket.
2: The 1948 Ashes series was a cricket rivalry between England and Australia.
Australia won 10 of their 12 lead-up matches, earning them the sobriquet of The Invincibles.
3: Don Tallon was a key member of Donald Bradman's famous Australian cricket team tour of England in 1948.
This unprecedented feat by a Test side touring England earned them the sobriquet The Invincibles.
4: The 1978 Gillette Cup Final was a cricket match between Somerset County Cricket Club and Sussex County Cricket Club.
It was the sixteenth final of the Gillette Cup.
5: Leah Poulton is a female Australian cricketer who plays for New South Wales and Australia.
She is a specialist batsman who usually opens the batting.
6: Norm O'Neill was a cricketer who played for New South Wales and Australia.
He was regarded as one of the finest fielders of his era.
7: Harry Fox played cricket and rugby for his county.
He played cricket for Somerset County Cricket Club from 1877 to 1882.

topic.name: Roller Coaster Design and History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, construction, and history of various roller coasters, particularly those manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard.

Document 1: Scream is a floorless roller coaster at Six Flags Magic Mountain.
Manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard, Scream was the park's sixteenth roller coaster.
Document 2: Afterburn is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Carowinds amusement park.
Designed by Bolliger & Mabillard, Afterburn stands 113 feet tall and reaches speeds of 62 miles per hour.
Document 3: X-Flight is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America.
Designed and built by Bolliger & Mabillard, the ride opened as the fourth Wing Coaster in the world.
Document 4: Bolliger & Mabillard is a roller coaster design consultancy based in Monthey, Switzerland.
Since 1988, B & M has built 100 roller coasters around the world.
Document 5: Intimidator is a steel roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard at Carowinds.
It is currently one of the tallest, fastest and longest roller coasters in the Southeast.

topic.name: Legal Frameworks and Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legal frameworks and rights pertaining to administrative law, equality, and naming conventions across different jurisdictions.

Document 1: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights.

The recognition of substantive legitimate expectations is somewhat controversial as it requires a balancing of the requirements of fairness against the reasons for any change in the authority's policy.
Document 5: Article 12 of the Constitution of the Republic of Singapore guarantees to all persons equality before the law and equal protection of the law.

Persons unable to show that one of the forbidden classifications applies to them may try to argue that they are members of a group defined by a law in a way that violates the general guarantee of equality and equal protection.

topic.name: College Sports and Athletes
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of college sports, focusing on teams, athletes, and notable events within NCAA competitions.

Document 1: Jahlil Okafor is an American professional basketball player for the Philadelphia 76ers of the NBA.
He played his freshman season of college for the 2014 – 15 Duke national championship team.
Document 2: The Georgetown Hoyas are the athletics teams that officially represent Georgetown University in college sports.
The men 's basketball team is the school 's most famous and most successful program.
Document 3: The 2009 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 2009 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
They played their home games at Michigan Stadium in Ann Arbor, Michigan and competed in the Big Ten Conference.
Document 5: The 2012 Sheraton Hawaii Bowl was a post-season American college football bowl game held on December 24, 2012.
It featured the SMU Mustangs from Conference USA against the Mountain West Conference co-champion Fresno State Bulldogs.
Document 7: The 2012 – 13 Big Ten men 's basketball season began with practices in October 2012.

topic.name: Fictional Characters in Horror and Sci-Fi
topic.description: This cluster explores various fictional characters from horror and science fiction media, highlighting their narratives and thematic significance.

Document 1: Sarah Barnes is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera Hollyoaks
Sarah's final storyline is her death in a skydiving accident, which was a first for a British soap opera.
Document 2: The Haunting is a 1963 British supernatural horror film directed and produced by Robert Wise
The film depicts the experiences of a small group of people invited by a paranormal investigator to investigate a purportedly haunted house.
Document 3: Vic Fontaine is a fictional character who appeared in the sixth season onwards of the American science fiction television series Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
Fontaine represented a source of romantic advice for the crew.
Document 4: Subspecies is an American, direct-to-video, horror film series produced by Full Moon Studios
The series followed the exploits of vampire Radu Vladislas.
Document 5: The Colour Out of Space is a short story written by American horror author H. P. Lovecraft
The narrator discovers that many years ago a meteorite crashed there, poisoning every living being nearby.
Document 6: Leprechaun is a 1993 American horror comedy film written and directed by Mark Jones
Davis plays a vengeful leprechaun who believes a family has stolen his pot of gold.
Document 7: Planet of the Ood is the third episode of the fourth series of British science fiction television series Doctor Who
The episode was well-received for its central theme of slavery.

topic.name: Art and Cultural Commentary
topic.description: This cluster discusses various artworks and their cultural significance, exploring themes of societal behavior, artistic influence, and personal expression.

Document 1: The painting is said to have been inspired by a frequent sight in Predić's home village of Orlovat — that of drunken youths returning from the pub at dawn.
The humorous content contributed to its popularity among critics, collectors and the public at large.
Document 2: Landscape painting in Scotland includes all forms of painting of landscapes in Scotland since its origins in the sixteenth century to the present day.
This period saw a shift in attitudes to the Highlands and mountain landscapes to interpreting them as aesthetically pleasing exemplars of nature.
Document 3: The Battle of Gettysburg, also known as the Gettysburg Cyclorama, is a cyclorama painting by the French artist Paul Philippoteaux depicting Pickett's Charge.
The intended effect is to immerse the viewer in the scene being depicted.
Document 4: Tipu's Tiger or Tippu's Tiger is an eighteenth-century automaton or mechanical toy created for Tipu Sultan, the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore in India.
The tiger was created for Tipu and makes use of his personal emblem of the tiger and expresses his hatred of his enemy, the British of the East India Company.
Document 5: Symphony in White, No. 3, is a painting by James Abbott McNeill Whistler.
By calling the painting Symphony in White, No. 3, Whistler intended to emphasise his artistic philosophy of corresponding arts.
Document 6: Philip Larkin was an English poet, novelist and librarian.
His poems are marked by what Andrew Motion calls a very English, glum accuracy about emotions, places, and relationships.
Document 7: Frost at Midnight is a poem by Samuel Taylor Coleridge, written in February 1798.
The poem expresses hope that Coleridge's son, Hartley, would be able to experience a childhood that his father could not.

Document 4: At least six dams have been planned on the Great Zab and its tributaries

the stream was dammed in the 1950s to create Lake Neepaulin

topic.name: Historical Events and Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss notable historical events and figures in American history, including space missions, prizefighting, and political incidents.

Document 1: Mercury-Atlas 8 was the fifth United States manned space mission, part of NASA's Mercury program.
This was the longest U.S. manned orbital flight yet achieved in the Space Race.
Document 3: Simon Byrne was an Irish bare-knuckle prize fighter.
Byrne fought in an era when English boxing was patronised by many powerful individuals.
Document 4: Mutiny of the Matoika is the common name for the events in July 1920 involving a large portion of the Olympic team of the United States.

topic.name: Music Albums and Releases
topic.description: The documents discuss various music albums, their production, and their impact in the music industry.

Document 1: Instant Karma ! is a song written by English musician John Lennon, released as a single on Apple Records in February 1970.
The song reached the top five in the British and American singles charts.
Document 2: Moog for Love is the seventh extended play recorded by British electronic music duo Disclosure.
The three-track record was released on 15 June 2016 by the labels Island and PMR Records.
Document 3: We 're New Here is a remix album by American recording artist Gil Scott-Heron and English music producer Jamie xx, released on February 21, 2011.
Jamie xx applied electronic music techniques in his production to remix Scott-Heron 's vocals from the original album over his own instrumentals.
Document 4: Stone Rollin ' is the fourth studio album by American recording artist Raphael Saadiq, released on March 25, 2011.
The album was noted for its stylistic breadth, groove-based compositions, varied subject matter, and incorporation of the Mellotron keyboard.
Document 5: The Albums 2000 – 2010 is a box set by Australian recording artist Kylie Minogue.
The five-disc box set was released in Europe, and Australasia, and included all of Minogue 's studio albums from the years 2000 to 2010.
Document 6: The Great Otis Redding Sings Soul Ballads is the second studio album by American soul singer-songwriter Otis Redding, released in 1965.

Document 7: New History Warfare Vol . 3 : To See More Light is the fourth studio album by Canada-based saxophonist Colin Stetson, released by Constellation Records in 2013.
It is the final part of a trilogy of albums that also encompasses New History Warfare Vol . 1 ( 2007 ) and New History Warfare Vol . 2 : Judges ( 2011 ).

topic.name: American Animal Breeds
topic.description: This topic encompasses various breeds of animals developed in the United States, focusing on their history, characteristics, and conservation efforts.

Document 1: The Nokota horse is a feral and semi-feral horse breed located in the badlands of southwestern North Dakota in the United States.
The breed developed in the 19th century from foundation bloodstock consisting of ranch-bred horses produced from the horses of local Native Americans mixed with Spanish horses.
Document 4: The Pony of the Americas (POA) is a pony breed developed in the state of Iowa in the United States.
A breed registry was founded in 1954, and within 15 years had registered 12,500 ponies.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss the history and notable events of professional wrestling, focusing on key figures, championships, and major wrestling promotions.

Document 1: Royal Rumble (2007) was the twentieth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The Undertaker, the thirtieth entrant, won the match by last eliminating Shawn Michaels.
Document 2: Gregory Helms is an American professional wrestler who is currently signed to Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
He is best known for his time with World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).

A founding member of the stable D-Generation X as the promotion's first female enforcer.

She is regarded by wrestling fans and wrestlers themselves as one of the greatest female wrestlers.

He competed from 1861 to 1891, traveling throughout the United States and Canada to face the top wrestlers of the day.
Document 6: Final Resolution (2006) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a tag team match pitting the team of Christian Cage and Sting against the team of Jeff Jarrett and Monty Brown.

topic.name: Abolition and Early American Politics
topic.description: The documents discuss key figures and events related to the abolition of slavery and political developments in early America.

Document 1: Levi Coffin was an American Quaker, abolitionist, and businessman deeply involved in the Underground Railroad.
Coffin's home is often called 'Grand Central Station of the Underground Railroad'.
Document 2: William Dummer served as lieutenant governor of Massachusetts during a period of conflict with native tribes.
Dummer's War was fought between British colonies and a coalition of native tribes.
Document 3: William S. Hamilton was a politician and miner who lived much of his life in Illinois and Wisconsin.
Hamilton served in various political offices and as a commander in two Midwest Indian Wars.
Document 4: George Madison served as the sixth Governor of Kentucky and was popular due to his military service in three wars.
Madison's political experience before becoming governor consisted of a twenty-year tenure as state auditor.
Document 5: Slavery in Indiana occurred between the time of French rule during late seventeenth century and 1826.
Opposition against slavery began to organize in Indiana around 1805, and abolitionists took control of the territorial legislature.
Document 6: The Overhill Cherokee were located in what is now Tennessee and rose to prominence in the early 18th century.
The decline of the Overhill towns was due to the westward thrust of Euro-American settlement.
Document 7: Ambrose Burnside was a Union Army general in the American Civil War and served as governor and a United States Senator.
Burnside's father was a slave owner who freed his slaves when he relocated to Indiana.

topic.name: Royal Australian Air Force History
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss the history, operations, and notable figures associated with the Royal Australian Air Force during World War II and beyond.

1: John Dundas was a British Second World War fighter pilot with No. 609 Squadron RAF.
Dundas claimed nine German aircraft shot down during the Battle of Britain.
2: No. 3 Aircraft Depot was formed in March 1942 at RAAF Station Amberley, Queensland.
The depot was responsible for heavy maintenance of the RAAF's English Electric Canberra jet bombers.
3: Western Area Command was formed in January 1941 and controlled RAAF units located in Western Australia.
Its aircraft conducted anti-submarine operations throughout the war.
5: Mary Bell was a founding leader of the Women’s Air Training Corps, which supported the RAAF during World War II.
She helped establish the Women’s Auxiliary Australian Air Force, the first women’s wartime service in Australia.
6: Haywood S. Hansell was a general officer in the United States Army Air Forces during World War II.
He was one of the chief architects of the concept of daylight precision bombing.
7: No. 391 Squadron was a Royal Australian Air Force squadron that operated during the Korean War.
It was responsible for administrative, logistical, medical, communications and security functions.

topic.name: Star Trek Series and Episodes
topic.description: The documents discuss various episodes and characters from the Star Trek television series, detailing their plots, character development, and production background.

Document 1: Ro Laren is a fictional character who appears on a recurring basis in the fifth, sixth and seventh series of Star Trek: The Next Generation.
The character first appears in the fifth season episode 'Ensign Ro'.
Document 3: 'The Trouble with Tribbles' is the 44th episode of the American science fiction television series, Star Trek.

Document 4: 'The Battle' is the eighth episode of the first season of Star Trek: The Next Generation.

Document 5: 'Where No One Has Gone Before' is the sixth episode of Star Trek: The Next Generation.
In 'Where No One Has Gone Before', the Enterprise is visited by Mr. Kosinski and an alien known as the Traveler.

topic.name: Naval Vessels of World War Era
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels used during the World War I and II eras, highlighting their designs, specifications, and historical significance.

Document 1: Russian submarine AG-22 was built for the Imperial Russian Navy during World War I.
AG-22 was abandoned and subsequently scrapped.
Document 2: Perun was an Uragan-class monitor built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s.
The ship was only active when the Gulf of Finland was not frozen.
Document 3: HMS Shannon served with the Home Fleet before World War I and participated in the Battle of Jutland.
She was paid off in 1919 and sold for scrap in 1922.
Document 4: The Admiral Hipper class comprised five heavy cruisers built by Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine in the mid-1930s.
Only the first three ships of the class saw action during World War II.
Document 5: HMS Dreadnought revolutionised naval power with her entry into service in 1906.
She was the first battleship to have a uniform main battery.
Document 6: SM U-68 was a Type U 66 submarine for the German Imperial Navy during the First World War.
She was sunk by a British Q-ship with all hands.
Document 7: SMS Irene was a protected cruiser of the German Imperial Navy and the lead ship of the Irene class.
She saw extensive service with the German fleet in the first years of her career.

topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of entertainment, including performance arts, music, and nightlife culture.

They have entertained as part of the Royal Variety Performance for England's Prince Charles.
Document 2: Three Sisters Tavern was a gay bar and strip club in Portland, Oregon.
The bar was considered a hub of Portland's nightlife before closing in 2004.
Document 3: 'The Way You Love Me' is a song recorded by American R & B singer-songwriter Keri Hilson.
The song was described as a reflection of Hilson's wilder side.

topic.name: Medieval Fortifications and Structures
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical significance, architectural features, and evolution of various medieval castles and fortifications across Europe.

Document 1: Loughor Castle is a ruined, medieval fortification located in the town of Loughor, Wales.
The castle was built around 1106 by the Anglo-Norman lord Henry de Beaumont, during the Norman invasion of Wales.
Document 2: St Mary 's Church, Reculver, was founded in the 7th century as either a minster or a monastery on the site of a Roman fort.
The original building, which incorporated stone and tiles scavenged from the Roman fort, was a simple one consisting only of a nave and an apsidal chancel.
Document 4: Lydford Castle is a medieval castle in the town of Lydford, Devon, England.
The second castle in Lydford was constructed in 1195 following a wave of law and order problems across England.
Document 5: The Newcastle town wall is a medieval defensive wall, and Scheduled Ancient Monument, in Newcastle upon Tyne, England.
It was built during the 13th and 14th centuries, and helped protect the town from attack and occupation during times of conflict.
Document 6: Roslin Castle is a partially ruined castle near the village of Roslin in Midlothian, Scotland.
There has been a castle on the site since the early 14th century, when the Sinclair family fortified the site.
Document 7: The Qal 'at al-Bahrain is an archaeological site located in Bahrain, on the Arabian Peninsula.
Archaeological excavations carried out since 1954 have unearthed antiquities from an artificial mound containing seven stratified layers.

topic.name: Military Conflicts in Europe
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various military conflicts and battles that shaped European history from the 14th to the 18th centuries.

Document 1: The Battle of Posada was fought between Basarab I of Wallachia and Charles I Robert of Hungary.
The battle resulted in a major Wallachian victory and disaster for Charles Robert.
Document 2: Artabanes was an East Roman general of Armenian origin who served under Justinian I.
He won great fame by killing the rebel general Guntharic and restoring the province to imperial allegiance.
Document 3: The Battle of Ohrid took place between the Ottoman Empire's forces and Skanderbeg's Albania.
Skanderbeg won the resulting battle and his men earned 40,000 ducats after captured Ottoman officers were ransomed.
Document 4: The Charles Town expedition was a combined French and Spanish attempt to capture Charles Town during Queen Anne's War.
Troops landed near Charles Town were quickly driven off by militia.
Document 5: Licario was a Byzantine admiral who reconquered many of the Aegean islands for Michael VIII Palaiologos.
He rose to the rank of megas konostaulos and megas doux.
Document 6: The Battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the American Revolutionary War.
The battles marked the outbreak of open armed conflict between Great Britain and thirteen colonies.
Document 7: The Battle of Höchstädt resulted in a French victory against the Austrians under Baron Pál Kray.
This opened the Danube pathway toward Vienna.

topic.name: Historical Business Developments
topic.description: This cluster discusses the historical evolution and significant events of various organizations across different industries.

Document 1: RTI International is a nonprofit organization founded in 1958 with funding by local businesses and universities.
RTI research has covered HIV / AIDS, healthcare, education curriculum and the environment.
Document 2: Michael Novogratz was a partner at Goldman Sachs and later an ex-hedge fund manager at Fortress Investment Group.
He was ranked a billionaire by Forbes in 2007 and 2008.
Document 3: Renault Agriculture was established in 1918 from its armored military vehicles division.
The company was sold between 2003 and 2008 to German rival Claas.
Document 4: Gulf Oil was a major global oil company from the 1900s to the 1980s.
Gulf ceased to exist as an independent company in 1985 when it merged with Standard Oil of California.
Document 5: John Thomas North was an English investor and businessman known as 'The King of Nitrates'.
He built monopolies in the Chilean nitrate industry during the War of the Pacific.
Document 6: Pearson's Candy Company was founded in 1909 as a confectionery distribution firm.
The company introduced its first confection, the Nut Goodie, in 1912.
Document 7: Timetoget was a railway company that tried to operate passenger trains on the Bratsberg Line in Norway.
The company was liquidated after failing to meet operational standards.

topic.name: Soviet and American Military Aircraft
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, development, and operational history of various military aircraft from the Soviet Union and the United States during and after World War II.

1: The Polikarpov I-185 was a Soviet fighter aircraft designed in 1940.
The I-185 program was cancelled on 27 January 1943.
2: The Ilyushin Il-6 was a Soviet long-range bomber developed from the Ilyushin Il-4 during 1942.
3: The Lockheed XF-104 was a single-engine, high-performance, supersonic interceptor prototype for a United States Air Force series.
The XF-104's maiden flight came in February 1954.
4: The Ilyushin Il-32 was a Soviet heavy military glider developed after World War II.
It was canceled when it became clear that no such tug was going to be available.
5: The Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II is a single seat, twin turbofan engine, straight wing jet aircraft developed by Fairchild-Republic for the United States Air Force.
The A-10 was designed for close-in support of ground troops.
7: The Bell Boeing V-22 Osprey is an American multi-mission, tiltrotor military aircraft.
The V-22 first flew in 1989, and began flight testing and design alterations.

topic.name: Medieval English Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss notable medieval figures from England and Hungary, focusing on their roles in politics, religion, and education.

Document 1: Robert of Melun was an English scholastic Christian theologian who taught in France.
He became Bishop of Hereford in England.
Document 2: Edward I was King of England from 1272 to 1307.
He spent much of his reign reforming royal administration and common law.
Document 3: Coloman was King of Hungary from 1095 and King of Croatia from 1097.
He allowed organized groups of crusaders to pass through Hungary peacefully.
Document 4: Emeric was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1196 and 1204.
He cooperated with the Holy See against the Bosnian Patarenes.
Document 5: Bosa was an Anglo-Saxon Bishop of York during the 7th and early 8th centuries.
He was educated at Whitby Abbey, where he became a monk.
Document 6: Ecgric was a king of East Anglia, the independent Anglo-Saxon kingdom.
He ruled the East Anglians together with Sigeberht.
Document 7: Simon of Southwell was a medieval English canon lawyer and canon.
He served in the household of Hubert Walter, Archbishop of Canterbury.

topic.name: Fungal Species Taxonomy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various species of fungi, their characteristics, habitats, and taxonomic classifications.

Document 1: Amanita ravenelii is a species of fungus in the family Amanitaceae.
It is widely distributed in mixed and deciduous forests of the southeastern United States.
Document 2: Pluteus nevadensis is a species of fungus in the agaric family Pluteaceae.
The species is known only from subtropical and pine forests in Mexico.
Document 3: Urnula craterium is a species of cup fungus in the family Sarcosomataceae.
It is parasitic on oak and various other hardwood species.
Document 4: Tricholoma vaccinum is a fungus of the agaric genus Tricholoma.
It grows in a mycorrhizal association with spruce or pine trees.
Document 5: Amanita abrupta is a species of fungus in the Amanitaceae family of mushrooms.
This terrestrial species grows in mixed woods in eastern North America and eastern Asia.
Document 6: Pulveroboletus bembae is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family.
The fungus grows in a mycorrhizal relationship with Gilbertiodendron dewevrei.
Document 7: Mycena polygramma is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
It is typically found on twigs or buried wood, recycling nutrients and forming humus.

topic.name: Athletes and the Olympics
topic.description: This topic encompasses the achievements, backgrounds, and participation of various athletes in the Olympic and Paralympic Games.

Document 1: Sam Oldham is an English artistic gymnast who represents Great Britain and was part of the British men's team at the 2012 Summer Olympics.
He is also a three-time junior European champion and won individual horizontal bar gold at the 2010 Summer Youth Olympics.
Document 2: The Rio de Janeiro bid for the 2016 Summer Olympics was a successful bid to host the Games of the XXXI Olympiad.
Rio de Janeiro was shortlisted receiving a 6.4 score, according to a study of its Application File delivered to the IOC.
Document 3: Britt Dillmann is a wheelchair basketball forward who won a silver medal at the 1988 Summer Paralympics in Seoul.
She staged a comeback in 2011 and won a gold medal at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London.
Document 4: Glencora Ralph is a member of the Australia women's national water polo team aiming to represent Australia in water polo at the 2012 Summer Olympics.
She has won gold medals at the 2011 Canada Cup and at the 2007 FINA Junior World Championships.
Document 5: Haiti sent a delegation to compete in the 2008 Summer Olympics, marking its seventh consecutive appearance at the summer Olympics.
The Haitian Olympic team included seven athletes participating in various sports.
Document 6: Para-snowboarding classification is a classification system for para-snowboarding, which made its official Winter Paralympic debut in the 2014 Winter Paralympics.
The sport is practiced by hundreds of athletes around the world.
Document 7: South Africa competed at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London with a team of 62 athletes.
Sprinter Oscar Pistorius was the most successful South African athlete winning individual gold in the T44 400 metres.

topic.name: Bach's Church Cantatas
topic.description: The documents discuss various church cantatas composed by Johann Sebastian Bach, highlighting their historical context, structure, and musical characteristics.

Document 1: Ich bin vergnügt mit meinem Glücke is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
Bach structured the work in five movements, alternating arias and recitatives.
Document 2: Christ unser Herr zum Jordan kam, BWV 7, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The cantata is scored for three vocal soloists, a four-part choir, two oboes d'amore, two solo violins, strings and basso continuo.
Document 4: Gottes Zeit ist die allerbeste Zeit, BWV 106, also known as Actus tragicus, is an early sacred cantata composed by Johann Sebastian Bach.
Bach scored the work for four vocal parts and a small ensemble of Baroque instruments.
Document 5: Jauchzet Gott in allen Landen, BWV 51, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The work is Bach's only church cantata scored for a solo soprano and trumpet.
Document 6: Barmherziges Herze der ewigen Liebe, BWV 185, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The cantata is structured in six movements begins with a duet, followed by a sequence of alternating arias and recitatives.
Document 7: Wir danken dir, Gott, wir danken dir, BWV 29, is a sacred cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The cantata was part of a festive service in the Nikolaikirche.

topic.name: Film and Cinema History
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of film and cinema, including notable actors, films, and their cultural impact.

Document 1: 12 Years a Slave is a 2013 period drama film and an adaptation of the 1853 slave narrative memoir Twelve Years a Slave by Solomon Northup
The film received widespread critical acclaim, and was named the best film of 2013 by several media outlets.
Document 2: Joan Crawford was an American film and television actress who started as a dancer and stage chorine.

Document 7: The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel is a 2011 British comedy-drama film, directed by John Madden.
The movie was produced by Participant Media and Blueprint Pictures on a budget of $10 million.

topic.description: The documents explore themes of success, personal development, and philosophical perspectives on individual potential and societal contributions.

the key to achieving world class expertise in any skill is a matter of practicing the correct way.

topic.name: Language and Communication
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of languages, including their structure, usage, and significance within different communities.

Document 3: The roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language (PIE) are basic parts of words that carry a lexical meaning.

Khmer has been influenced considerably by Sanskrit and Pali, especially in the royal and religious registers.

topic.name: Rowing and Motorsports Events
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable annual rowing competitions and motorsport races, highlighting key events, participants, and outcomes.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Oxford won by seven lengths, their biggest margin of victory for more than 90 years.

The race was won by McLaren driver Mika Häkkinen, who started from the pole position.

Oxford won by two-and-a-quarter lengths.

Oxford won by three-and-a-half lengths.

The race was won by Oxford, their first Boat Race victory at Easter.
Document 7: The 2010 Showtime Southern 500 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series motor race held at Darlington Raceway.
The race was won by Denny Hamlin for Joe Gibbs Racing after starting seven positions behind polesitter Jamie McMurray.

topic.name: State Highways and Roadways
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways and roadways in the United States, detailing their routes, histories, and significance in their respective regions.

Document 1: Maryland Route 413 is a 14.61-mile state highway in Somerset County in the U.S. state of Maryland.
MD 413 is part of two scenic routes: Blue Crab Scenic Byway and the Beach to Bay Indian Trail.
Document 2: M-205 was a state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan.
MDOT swapped roadways with the Cass County Road Commission ending the 67-year history of M-205.
Document 3: The Adams Avenue Parkway is a one-half-mile-long private toll road in the U.S. state of Utah.
This allowed commuters to bypass the rest of US-89 through Washington Terrace to I-84.
Document 4: M-129 is a state trunkline highway in the Upper Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
The highway forms part of the larger Lake Huron Circle Tour.
Document 5: H-63 is a county-designated highway in the Upper Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
The road is called Mackinac Trail after the Upper Peninsula branch of an Indian trail.
Document 6: U.S. Route 13 Business is an 8.19-mile business route of US 13 that runs through the heart of Wilmington in New Castle County, Delaware.
US 13 was designated to run through downtown Wilmington and along Philadelphia Pike in 1926.
Document 7: M-143 is a previously unsigned spur state trunkline highway in the south central region of the US state of Michigan.
Michigan Avenue travels through the Greater Lansing area on an east-west axis.

topic.name: Birds and Sharks
topic.description: This cluster discusses various species of birds and sharks, highlighting their characteristics, habitats, and behaviors.

Document 1: The crag martins are four species of small passerine birds in the genus Ptyonoprogne of the swallow family.
These are species of craggy mountainous habitats.
Document 2: The whitetip reef shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae.
It is typically found on or near the bottom in clear water.
Document 3: The smoothtooth blacktip shark is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae.
Little is known of the smoothtooth blacktip shark's natural history.
Document 4: The tiger catshark is a species of catshark, belonging to the family Scyliorhinidae.
It can additionally be identified by its dorsal colour pattern of ten dark brown saddles.
Document 5: The Virgin Islands dwarf sphaero or Virgin Islands dwarf gecko is a species of gecko and also one of the smallest terrestrial vertebrates.
Document 6: The dusky crag martin is a small passerine bird in the swallow family.
This martin nests under a cliff overhang or on a man-made structure.
Document 7: The fieldfare is a member of the thrush family Turdidae.
It breeds in woodland and scrub in northern Europe and Asia.

topic.name: Plant Biology and Ecology

topic.name: Military History and Honors
topic.description: This cluster focuses on notable military figures, units, and their contributions during major conflicts in the 20th century.

Document 1: Cornelius H. Charlton was a soldier in the United States Army during the Korean War.
Charlton posthumously received the Medal of Honor for his actions near Chipo-ri, South Korea on June 2, 1951.
Document 2: The 6th Division was an infantry division of the Australian Army.
It served in the North African Campaign, the Greek campaign, on Crete and in Syria.
Document 3: Howard Kippenberger was an officer of the New Zealand Military Forces who served in the First and Second World Wars.
He led the battalion for two years, through the Battles of Greece and Crete.
Document 4: The 189th Infantry Brigade is an Infantry brigade of the United States Army based at Joint Base Lewis-McChord, Washington.
It has been used principally as a training unit for most of its existence.
Document 5: The Croatian National Guard was established during the Croatian War of Independence.
It was tasked with the protection of Croatia's borders and territory.
Document 6: The Royal Australian Regiment is the parent administrative regiment for regular infantry battalions of the Australian Army.
Units of the Royal Australian Regiment have deployed on operations in Japan, Korea, Malaya, Borneo, Vietnam, Somalia, Rwanda, Cambodia, East Timor, the Solomon Islands, Iraq and Afghanistan.
Document 7: Lieutenant General Sir Alwyn Ragnar Garrett was a senior commander in the Australian Army.
He served as Chief of the General Staff from 1958 to 1960.

topic.name: Classic Video Games
topic.description: This cluster focuses on notable classic video games, their gameplay mechanics, and critical reception.

Document 1: Cobra Triangle is a 1989 racing, vehicular combat video game developed by Rare and released by Nintendo for the Nintendo Entertainment System.
IGN and GamesRadar ranked Cobra Triangle among their top NES games.
Document 2: Agar.io is a massively multiplayer action game created by Matheus Valadares.
The game was released to positive critical reception; critics particularly praised its simplicity, competition, and mechanics.
Document 3: Brain Age 2 is an edutainment puzzle game and the sequel to Brain Age: Train Your Brain in Minutes a Day!
Critics were generally favorable towards Brain Age 2, which received aggregated scores of 77% from Metacritic.
Document 4: Resident Evil 4 is a survival horror third-person shooter video game developed and published by Capcom.
The story of Resident Evil 4 follows the U.S. government special agent Leon S. Kennedy.
Document 5: In 1982, Atari Inc. released a port of Namco's hit arcade game Pac-Man for its Atari 2600 video game console.
The port is the best selling Atari 2600 game of all time, selling an estimated 7 million copies.
Document 6: Neutopia is an overhead action-adventure video game developed by Hudson Soft.
Neutopia is widely regarded as a clone of the popular Nintendo game The Legend of Zelda in nearly every aspect.
Document 7: Kid Icarus is an action platform video game for the Family Computer Disk System in Japan and the Nintendo Entertainment System in Europe and North America.
The plot of Kid Icarus revolves around protagonist Pit's quest for three sacred treasures.

topic.name: Political Leadership and Governance
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of political leadership and governance across different countries and historical contexts.

Document 1: The events surrounding the formation of the United Kingdom's government in 2010 took place between 7 May and 12 May 2010
the first hung parliament in the UK in 36 years, sparking a series of negotiations which would form the first coalition government since the Second World War.

Lincoln's mostly successful attempts to reconcile conflicting personalities and political factions on the path to abolition and victory in the American Civil War.
Document 4: Julia Gillard is a former Australian politician who served as the 27th Prime Minister of Australia from 2010 to 2013
She was the first and to date only woman to hold the positions of deputy prime minister, prime minister and leader of a major party in Australia.
Document 6: Arthur Schultz was an American Republican politician and a five-term mayor of Joliet, Illinois
Joliet changed from an economy of a prison town with declining population to a fast-growing entertainment and tourism economy.

topic.name: Mid-20th Century Media and Culture
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evolution and impact of various media forms, including magazines, literature, and entertainment, in the mid-20th century.

Document 1: Space Science Fiction Magazine was a US science fiction magazine published by Republic Features Syndicate
the magazine ceased publication late in 1957.
Document 2: Chris Metzen is an American game designer, artist, voice actor and author known for his work creating the fictional universes and scripts for Blizzard Entertainment's three major award-winning media franchises
Metzen is currently the Senior Vice President of Story and Franchise Development at Blizzard Entertainment.
Document 4: The Diamond Smugglers is a non-fiction work by Ian Fleming that was first published in 1957
the book is based on two weeks of interviews Fleming undertook with John Collard.

topic.name: Meteorological Phenomena and Physics
topic.description: This topic encompasses various atmospheric phenomena and their physical principles, including wake lows, hail formation, and the contributions of notable physicists to experimental science.

Document 1: Wake low , or wake depression , is a mesoscale low-pressure area which trails the mesoscale high following a squall line.
Severe weather , in the form of high winds , can be generated by the wake low when the pressure difference between the mesohigh preceding it and the wake low is intense enough.
Document 7: Hail is a form of solid precipitation.
Hail formation requires environments of strong , upward motion of air with the parent thunderstorm.

topic.name: Political History and Movements
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant political figures, movements, and events that shaped nations and ideologies throughout the 20th century and beyond.

1: Alphonse Alley was a Beninese army officer and political figure.
His administration oversaw the creation of a new constitution and a presidential election.
2: Hezbollah is a Shi'a Islamist militant group and political party based in Lebanon.
Hezbollah was conceived by Muslim clerics and funded by Iran primarily to harass the Israeli occupation.
3: Hafizullah Amin was an Afghan politician and statesman during the Cold War.
He was one of the leading organisers of the Saur Revolution which overthrew the government of Mohammad Daoud Khan.
4: The Women's March on Versailles was one of the earliest and most significant events of the French Revolution.
The march symbolized a new balance of power that displaced the ancient privileged orders of the French nobility.
5: The Cultural Revolution Group played a central role in the Cultural Revolution's first few years.
Mao Zedong called on the leaders of the Chinese Communist Party to implement a 'Cultural Revolution' in China.
6: Tom Kahn was an American social democrat known for his leadership in several organizations.
He was an activist and influential strategist in the Civil Rights Movement.
7: Independence Day in India commemorates the nation's independence from the British Empire.
India attained independence following an Independence Movement noted for largely nonviolent resistance and civil disobedience.

topic.name: American Detective Television Series
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various American television series that revolve around detective work, mystery-solving, and the exploration of complex characters and narratives.

Document 1: Mr. Monk and the Candidate is the two-part pilot episode of the American comedy-drama detective television series Monk.
Monk investigates an assassination attempt on a mayoral candidate.
Document 2: The X-Files centers on FBI special agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully who work on cases linked to the paranormal, called X-Files.
Gethsemane explores the overarching mythology of The X-Files.
Document 3: Veronica Mars depicts the adventures of Veronica Mars as she deals with life as a college student while moonlighting as a private detective.
In Spit & Eggs, Veronica enlists the help of her friends in patrolling a party to look for date rape drugs.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricane History
topic.description: A comprehensive overview of significant hurricanes and hurricane seasons that have impacted the Atlantic region, detailing their formation, paths, impacts, and historical significance.

Document 1: The 1821 Norfolk and Long Island Hurricane was one of four known tropical cyclones that have made landfall in New York City.
It moved ashore near Wilmington, North Carolina, and passed near Norfolk, Virginia before moving through the Delmarva Peninsula and New Jersey.
Document 2: The 1925 Atlantic hurricane season was an inactive Atlantic hurricane season during which four tropical cyclones formed.
The last storm caused severe damage along the beaches of the Florida Peninsula, with damages estimated in the millions along with four fatalities near Tampa.
Document 3: The Great Hurricane of 1780 is the deadliest Atlantic hurricane on record.
Between 20,000 and 22,000 people died throughout the Lesser Antilles when the storm passed through them from October 10 – 16.
Document 4: The 1972 Atlantic hurricane season had only four fully tropical named storms – the fewest since 1930.
The most significant storm of the season was Hurricane Agnes, which at the time was the costliest United States hurricane.
Document 5: Hurricane Sandra in 2015 was the latest-forming major hurricane in the Northeastern Pacific basin on record.
This made Sandra the strongest November hurricane on record in the Northeastern Pacific.
Document 6: Hurricane Ignacio was the latest-forming first hurricane of a Pacific hurricane season since reliable satellite observation began in 1966.
The impact of Ignacio was compounded by flooding from Hurricane Marty a month later.
Document 7: Hurricane Donna brought severe damage to the Lesser Antilles, the Greater Antilles, and the East Coast of the United States.
In Puerto Rico, severe flash flooding led to 107 fatalities, 85 of them in Humacao alone.

topic.name: Cultural and Educational Institutions

the city has a reputation for progressive politics, public art, and powerful journalism
